mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing equity markets to price in lower future earnings and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and short-term economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading horizon.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when applied outside early-cycle expansion regimes or when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price index growth exceeding 3.0% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.0%, or Rank IC turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings to generate positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive returns when GDP growth falls below 1.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly gross domestic product levels indicate stronger aggregate economic output and corporate revenue generation, directly translating to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals an acute supply-side contraction or demand shock, prompting immediate equity deleveraging and negative forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns fail to materialize in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal generates false positives during temporary seasonal adjustments.
